entities.name: DIETARY DIVERSITY
entities.description: Measure of the variety of foods consumed by households

entities.name: FOOD COPING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Actions taken by households to manage food shortages

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of a household to withstand and recover from shocks

entities.name: FOOD INSECURITY STATUS
entities.description: The state of lacking reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: PROPORTIONATE SAMPLING TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A sampling method used to interview 3440 households across 18 districts

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where the study was conducted

entities.name: ADAPTIVE RESILIENCE CAPACITIES
entities.description: The ability to adjust strategies to maintain food security

entities.name: ABSORPTIVE RESILIENCE CAPACITIES
entities.description: The ability to use reserves or assets to cope with shocks

entities.name: INCOME
entities.description: Financial resources available to the household

relationships.description: Resilient households showed better diet diversification
Food secure status is associated with better diet diversification
Higher resilience reduces the likelihood of adopting food coping strategies
Food insecurity increases the likelihood of adopting food coping strategies
Adaptive capacities reduce the use of food coping strategies
Absorptive capacities reduce the use of food coping strategies
Higher income reduces the use of food coping strategies
Sampling technique applied to households within Zimbabwe
